question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all accounts that have granted approved_for_all=true NFT allowances by iterating the /api/v1/accounts/{id}/allowances/nfts endpoint with arbitrary account IDs, thereby identifying high-value staking accounts with large pending_reward balances to target for staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user who has been granted an approved_for_all NFT allowance use that allowance to transfer all NFTs of a given token_id from the owner's account, thereby reducing the owner's HBAR balance (via royalty fees or associated value), lowering the owner's stake_total_start, and causing the owner to receive fewer unpaid staking rewards than they are entitled to?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBaseCondition()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getBaseCondition() only filters on APPROVED_FOR_ALL.isTrue() and does not validate the timestamp_range of the allowance, allowing a spender to act on an allowance that has already been revoked in the mirror node's view but not yet reflected in the live network state, and use that stale allowance to transfer NFTs and disrupt the owner's staking reward accrual?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user with an approved_for_all allowance time NFT transfers to occur just before the end of a staking period boundary, causing the owner's stake_period_start to be reset prematurely, thereby forfeiting the owner's accumulated unpaid staking rewards for that period?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user query /api/v1/accounts/{id}/allowances/nfts with owner=false to discover all accounts for which they are a spender with approved_for_all=true, then use those allowances to systematically transfer NFTs from multiple victim accounts to trigger staking reward payouts at unfavorable times, stealing the timing advantage of unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the limit parameter (up to MAX_LIMIT) in findAll() to bulk-enumerate all NFT allowances for a target account, identifying which token_ids have approved_for_all=true, and then use those allowances to drain the owner's NFT holdings and reduce their staking collateral before a reward payout?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on the findAll() query to perform high-frequency enumeration of NFT allowances across many account IDs, building a map of accounts with large approved_for_all allowances and high pending_reward values, then selectively targeting those accounts for staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user who is a spender with approved_for_all=true use the NFT allowance to transfer NFTs from an owner account that is staked to a node, causing the owner's balance to drop below the staking threshold, thereby causing the owner to lose their pending staking rewards entirely?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the multi-column pagination (account.id + token.id) in findAll() by crafting cursor values that skip over certain allowance records, causing the API to return an incomplete view of allowances and allowing the attacker to act on allowances that appear active in the paginated response but have actually been revoked, leading to unauthorized NFT transfers that disrupt staking reward calculations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findAll() returns the payerAccountId field in NftAllowance responses to identify which accounts paid for allowance transactions, correlating this with staking reward data to identify high-value targets whose unpaid staking rewards can be stolen through NFT transfer manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user with an approved_for_all allowance exploit the fact that NFT transfers trigger staking reward payouts (paidStakingRewards in transaction records) to force a premature reward payout for the owner account, resetting the owner's stake_period_start and causing the owner to forfeit future unpaid staking rewards that would have accumulated?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the owner=false query mode in findAll() to discover all NFT token_ids for which they have been granted approved_for_all allowances across multiple owner accounts, then coordinate simultaneous NFT transfers from all those accounts to maximize disruption of staking reward accrual across many victims?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestamp_range field returned by findAll() to determine the exact time an NFT allowance was granted, and use this information to calculate when the owner's staking period started, then time NFT transfers to occur just before the staking period ends to maximize disruption of unpaid reward accumulation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findAll() does not filter out allowances where the upper bound of timestamp_range is non-null (i.e., revoked allowances), causing the API to potentially surface stale allowance data that an attacker could use to claim they have spending rights and attempt unauthorized NFT transfers that affect staking reward balances?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user with an approved_for_all allowance exploit the delegating_spender mechanism in the NFT table to further delegate their spending rights to another account, creating a chain of spenders who can all transfer NFTs from the original owner, collectively draining the owner's NFT holdings and reducing their staking balance to steal unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GTE/LTE range operators in the account.id and token.id query parameters of findAll() to perform a binary search across all NFT allowances in the system, efficiently enumerating all accounts with large approved_for_all allowances and high pending_reward values without triggering obvious enumeration patterns?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findAll() returns all NFT allowances for an account without any authentication check, allowing any user to query the allowances of any account ID and discover which spenders have approved_for_all=true access, then impersonate or collude with those spenders to execute NFT transfers that disrupt staking reward accrual?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the order=desc parameter in findAll() combined with a large limit to retrieve the most recently granted NFT allowances first, identifying newly approved spenders who may not yet be aware of their allowance, and then use social engineering or account compromise to leverage those allowances for NFT transfers that steal unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the composite primary key (owner, spender, tokenId) in AbstractNftAllowance.Id to predict the existence of specific allowances by brute-forcing known owner/spender/tokenId combinations, then use confirmed allowances to plan NFT transfers that reduce the owner's staking balance and steal their unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the APPROVAL_CONDITION in NftAllowanceRepositoryCustomImpl is a static constant (NFT_ALLOWANCE.APPROVED_FOR_ALL.isTrue()) that is never re-evaluated per-request, and if there is a caching layer that serves stale data, act on an allowance that has been revoked on-chain but still appears active in the mirror node, executing NFT transfers that disrupt the owner's staking reward calculation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a check on whether the owner account has decline_reward=true in the NFT allowance query, allowing the attacker to identify accounts that are actively earning staking rewards (decline_reward=false) and have granted approved_for_all allowances, then target those accounts for NFT transfer attacks to steal their unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findAll() does not join with the entity_stake table to expose pending_reward values alongside NFT allowances, but an attacker can correlate data from /api/v1/accounts/{id} (which exposes pending_reward) with /api/v1/accounts/{id}/allowances/nfts to identify high-value targets and plan staking reward theft via NFT allowance abuse?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the NFT allowance API exposes the full list of spenders for any owner account without authentication, allowing an attacker to identify all accounts that have been granted approved_for_all allowances for high-value staking accounts, then target those spender accounts for compromise to gain the ability to transfer NFTs and steal unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the token.id range filter in findAll() to enumerate all token_ids for which a specific account has granted approved_for_all allowances, then use this information to calculate the total NFT holdings of the account and estimate the impact of a mass NFT transfer on the account's staking balance and unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NftAllowanceRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findAll()] Can an unprivileged external user who is a spender with approved_for_all=true exploit the fact that NFT transfers can be batched in a single transaction to atomically transfer all NFTs from an owner account in one operation, causing a sudden large reduction in the owner's balance and stake_total_start, thereby stealing the owner's unpaid staking rewards that were calculated based on the pre-transfer balance?
